[1;2m› [0mNice. Whats your view for the current state of the game? Does it makes      
  sense. Is it coherrent? ANy clear issues with the design?                   
                                                                              
                                                                              
[2m• [0mYes, it makes sense, and it is becoming coherent. The core loop is now much 
  clearer than before because Realignment gives the game an actual macro-     
  structure.                                                                  
                                                                              
  The game currently has three connected clocks:                              
                                                                              
  - [1mOffer / Realignment clock:[0m rune offer empties, forcing upkeep.            
  - [1mTension clock:[0m if nobody releases, Contraction advances domains anyway.   
  - [1mDomain instability clock:[0m shard removal eventually flips resonance and    
    pushes death pressure.                                                    
                                                                              
  That is a strong foundation. It means players cannot just sit and optimize  
  forever. Releasing, not releasing, dying, and drafting all feed back into   
  the same endgame system.                                                    
                                                                              
  The best part of the design right now is that aggression has a nuanced cost.
  Releasing is not just “attack someone”; it also relieves tension, starts a  
  new spell, consumes shards, gains resonance, and moves the world toward     
  instability. That gives aggressive play a real identity: borrow power,      
  accelerate the game, risk burning out.                                      
                                                                              
  The clearest design strengths:                                              
                                                                              
  - [1mRealignment is doing useful work.[0m It refreshes, pressures, kills, and     
    advances the game.                                                        
                                                                              
  - [1mResonance flipping both ways is interesting.[0m It creates room for          
    manipulation rather than a one-way punishment track.                      
                                                                              
  - [1mContraction is a good anti-stall rule.[0m It prevents pure control tables    
    from refusing to engage.                                                  
                                                                              
  - [1mCrack Event gives death a board impact.[0m Eliminated players leave          
    instability behind, which should help close the game.                     
                                                                              
  - [1mFormula / Incantation split is much clearer.[0m The vocabulary is moving in  
    the right direction.                                                      
                                                                              
  Clear design risks:                                                         
                                                                              
  [94m1. [39;1mBookkeeping could get heavy[0m                                              
     Active zone icons, domain board icons, resonance faces, draw icons, focus
     tokens, spell decks, status decks, shards, calm zone shards. This can    
     work, but only if physical layout is very clean.                         
                                                                              
  [94m2. [39;1mDraw icon chaining may be dangerous[0m                                      
     “Draw while you have cards to draw” is elegant, but it can create swingy 
     refreshes. It may be fine, but it needs early testing.                   
                                                                              
  [94m3. [39;1mContraction may be too punishing at 4 players[0m                            
     Advancing all four domains and giving everyone resonance is dramatic. I  
     like it as an anti-stall mechanism, but it may make one no-release round 
     feel catastrophic.                                                       
                                                                              
  [94m4. [39;1mControl archetype needs active agency[0m                                    
     Control can wait, but it still needs meaningful actions besides “do not  
     release.” Ripple manipulation, shard redirection, resonance flipping,    
     formula denial, or deck filtering could fill this.
